title: GOD-OPENCODE opencode.json
description: Schema for the opencode.json file at the root of a GOD-OPENCODE project. Validates the agent roster, slash commands, instruction glob patterns, permission rules, and (v1.3+) MCP server registrations.

$schema.description: JSON Schema URL for editor autocomplete + validation.

instructions.description: Files or glob patterns loaded into agent context at startup.

permission.description: Per-tool permission rules merged into global ~/.config/opencode/opencode.jsonc on install.

agent.description: Map of agent name -> agent definition. Each name must match agents/<name>/AGENT.md in the project tree. Keys must be lowercase letters, digits, and hyphens (start with letter).

command.description: Map of slash command name -> command definition. Each name must match commands/<name>.md in the project tree. Keys must be lowercase letters, digits, and hyphens (start with letter).

mcp_servers.description: Map of MCP server name -> server config. Each registered server is wrapped as skills/<category>/mcp-<name>/SKILL.md by scripts/mcp-to-skill.ps1. Note: top-level key uses snake_case to match OpenCode's host spec; other top-level keys use lowercase-only.

prompt.description: Persona prompt for the agent. Concatenated with skills listed in agents/<name>/AGENT.md at runtime.

tools.description: OpenCode tool allowlist. If absent, all standard tools are available.

skills.description: Skills this agent can call. Optional; the agent-router derives defaults from the auto-router skill if absent.

agent.description: Agent invoked when the command is run. If absent, auto-router selects.

transport.description: MCP transport. stdio runs a subprocess, http/sse are remote endpoints.

command.description: Executable to spawn for stdio transport (e.g. 'npx', 'uvx', '~/.local/bin/foo'). Required only when transport=stdio.

url.description: Endpoint URL for http/sse transports. Required when transport is http or sse.

category.description: Skills category under which the generated wrapper SKILL.md is placed by scripts/mcp-to-skill.ps1.

skills_description.description: One-line summary used in the generated SKILL.md wrapper body.

backend-engineer.prompt: You are a senior backend engineer...

security-audit.prompt: Load the auto-router skill and execute the security-audit workflow.

filesystem.skills_description: Read/write files in the project tree.
